name: Crystal Candy
recipeIngredient: a few drops oil of cinnamon, oil of peppermint, or oil of wintergreen
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8x8x2 baking pan with foil, extending foil over the edges of the pan.
2. Butter the foil and set the pan aside.
3. Butter the sides of a heavy 3-quart saucepan.
4. In the saucepan, combine sugar, corn syrup, and water.
5. Cook over medium-high heat until boiling, about 5 minutes, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon to dissolve the sugar.
6. Avoid splashing the mixture on the sides of the pan.
7. Carefully attach a candy thermometer to the side of the pan.
8. Continue cooking over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the thermometer registers 290°F (soft-crack stage), about 20-25 minutes. The mixture should boil at a moderate, steady rate over the entire surface.
9. Remove the saucepan from the heat and carefully remove the thermometer.
10. Quickly stir in the desired food coloring and flavoring.
11. Immediately pour the mixture into the prepared pan.
12. Let stand 5-10 minutes or until a film forms over the surfaces of the candy.
13. Using a broad spatula, gently press lines across the surface, about 1/2 inch from one edge of the pan.
14. Do not break through the film on the surface. Repeat pressing along the other three edges of the pan, intersecting at the corners to form squares.
15. If the candy does not hold its shape, it is not cool enough. Let stand a few minutes and start again.
16. Continue marking lines along all sides, about 1/2 inch apart, until you reach the center.
17. Retrace the lines, pressing the spatula deeper without breaking the film on the surface.
18. Repeat until the spatula can be pressed to the bottom along all lines.
19. Cool completely. Use foil to lift the candy out of the pan and break into squares.
20. Store covered. Makes about 1 1/2 lbs of candy.
21. To make molded candies, oil hard candy molds. Quickly pour the mixture into molds.
22. Cool for 10 minutes or until firm.
23. Invert the molds and twist to release the candies.
24. Cool completely.
